outbound_official.note: no official Gambia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Searched mofa.gov.gm (Ministry of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Gambians Abroad), gid.gov.gm (Gambia Immigration Department), and gambia.gov.gm (The Official Website of The Government of The Gambia). All official pages exclusively publish inbound visa requirements (who may enter The Gambia), not visa-free travel privileges for Gambia ordinary-passport holders traveling abroad. No dedicated outbound travel advisory or country access list was found on any official Gambia government domain.

Listed under 'Countries With Visa Exemption Agreement' on gid.gov.gm. Confirmed by gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ and gambiahc.org.uk: 'do not require a visa to enter the country whether on holiday or on a business trip not exceeding 90 days.' NOTE: UK FCDO (gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/the-gambia/) states the initial arrival stamp is 28 days, extendable twice by 28 days each at the Immigration Office, for a total of up to 84 days; the Gambia HC in London states the overall limit is 90 days. The 90-day figure is the stated Gambian policy ceiling; the 28-day initial stamp reflects the immigration officer practice at port of entry.
Listed under 'Countries With Visa Exemption Agreement' on official GID visa page.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'SCHENGEN/EUROPEAN UNION CITIZENS INCLUDING THE UNITED KINGDOM AND SWITZERLAND' and Section IV: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states including Great Britain and Switzerland... can travel to The Gambia visa free'). Switzerland does NOT appear on the gid.gov.gm/visa/ enumerated list - sourced exclusively from the Gambia High Commission in Senegal official diplomatic document. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS'). CONFLICT: gid.gov.gm/visa/ places UAE under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance (Asia and Middle East)'. The HC Senegal PDF (dated Feb 2026) is more recent; it is an official Gambia diplomatic mission document. UAE status recorded here as visa-free based on the newer document. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary and gid.gov.gm SSL error); entry preserved per safety rules. Travelers should verify directly with Gambia immigration before travel.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS'). Turkey did not appear in any category on gid.gov.gm/visa/ in the prior record. The HC Senegal PDF (dated Feb 2026) is an official Gambia diplomatic mission document. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules. Travelers should verify directly with Gambia immigration before travel.

gambia.gov.gm states: 'Citizens of... full members of the European Union... do not require a visa to enter the country; whether on holiday or on a business trip not exceeding 90 days.' Confirmed again from gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ on 2026-06-27. The Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'SCHENGEN/EUROPEAN UNION CITIZENS'; Section IV: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states...') confirms this as a non-reciprocal waiver applying to ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic passports without requiring clearance.

gambia.gov.gm states: 'Citizens of... the Commonwealth... do not require a visa to enter the country; whether on holiday or on a business trip not exceeding 90 days.' Confirmed again from gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ and gambiahc.org.uk on 2026-06-27. Also confirmed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'COMMONWEALTH CITIZENS'). Individual Commonwealth countries not enumerated on these pages.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'AU CITIZENS') as visa-exempt. This encompasses all 55 AU member states. Note: several specific African countries appear in the 'Both Visa And Clearance' category on gid.gov.gm/visa/ (e.g. Sudan, Chad, DRC, South Africa, Egypt, Cameroon, etc.) - there is a direct conflict between the Feb 2026 HC Senegal document and the gid.gov.gm country list. The newer document is recorded here. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules. Travelers should verify before travel.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'NORDIC STATES') as visa-exempt. Covers Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden (Finland, Iceland, and Sweden are also individually enumerated on gid.gov.gm/visa/). Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'BALTIC AND BALKAN STATES') as visa-exempt. Baltics would include Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania. Balkans typically include Albania, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, Slovenia, Romania, Kosovo. NOTE: several of these (Albania, Belarus, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Georgia, Lithuania, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine) appear under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance For European Countries' on gid.gov.gm/visa/. Significant conflict with gid.gov.gm. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules. Travelers should verify.

visa_free.nationality: Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators (group label)
visa_free.notes: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'TOURIST ON CHARTERED FLIGHTS AND TOUR OPERATORS') as visa-exempt, holding any passport type (ordinary, official/service, or diplomatic) per Section IV. gambia.gov.gm entry-requirement page notes tourists on last-minute bookings must submit passport to Immigration within 48 hours for a proper visa - this may apply to non-exempt nationalities. Could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation (PDF loaded as binary); entry preserved per safety rules.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: All nationalities (where no Gambian diplomatic mission is present in home country)
visa_on_arrival.notes: GID entry requirements page states: 'Visa can be acquired from all Gambian Embassies and some Consulates abroad as well as Visa upon arrival at the border.' gambia.gov.gm states: 'Where there is no Gambian diplomatic representative, participants shall have the privilege to obtain entry visa at the Banjul International Airport upon arrival.' Short visit visa valid up to 90 days. Note: countries that require both visa AND clearance must also have pre-approved entry clearance valid for 30 days before arrival.

visa_policy.notes: The official GID visa page (gid.gov.gm/visa/) divides countries into five categories: (1) ECOWAS free entry - 11 member states; (2) Countries with visa exemption agreement - 18 listed: Belgium, Finland, Poland, Sweden, Canada, Germany, Iceland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, San Marino, Spain, Tunisia, Indonesia, Russia, Australia, United Kingdom, Czech Republic; (3) Countries needing both visa AND entry clearance - African group: Sudan, South Sudan, Chad, Central African Republic, DRC, Congo Brazzaville, Gabon, Angola, South Africa, Lesotho, Cameroon, Mozambique, Burundi, Rwanda, Somalia, Algeria, Libya, Ethiopia, Djibouti, Eritrea, Madagascar, Comoros Island, Botswana, Equatorial Guinea, Egypt; European group: Albania, Belarus, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Georgia, Lithuania, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine; Asia/Middle East group: Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Brunei, Cambodia, China, India, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Laos, Mongolia, Myanmar, Nepal, North Korea, Oman, Pakistan, Palestine, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, UAE, Uzbekistan, Yemen; Americas/Caribbean: Bahamas, Bolivia, Chile, Bermuda, Colombia, Haiti; (4) Countries needing only clearance - African: Kenya, Malawi, Mauritius, Namibia, Sao Tome & Principe, Seychelles, Tanzania, Uganda, Zimbabwe, Eswatini; Asia: East Timor, Indonesia, Malaysia, Maldives, Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam, Brunei Darussalam; Americas/Caribbean: Antigua & Barbuda, Barbados, Anguilla, Aruba, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Grenada, Guyana, Honduras, Jamaica, St Kitts & Nevis, St Lucia, St Vincent & Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad & Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela, Cayman Islands, Guadeloupe, Martinique, Montserrat, Netherlands Antilles, Puerto Rico; Pacific: Fiji, Kiribati, Nauru, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu; (5) Countries needing only visa (no clearance) - African: Morocco, Swaziland, Zambia; European: Andorra, Austria, France, Liechtenstein, Malta, Portugal, Switzerland, Hungary, Romania, Greece; Americas: Argentina, Belize, Brazil, British Virgin Islands, Costa Rica, Cuba, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, USA; Pacific: New Zealand (and several Indonesian/Australian geographic regions listed in apparent error: Flores, Lombok, Melanesia, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Sulawesi, Sumbawa, Tasmania, Timor). A Feb 2026 'New Visa Policy' document issued by the Gambia High Commission in Senegal (official diplomatic mission) significantly expands the visa-exempt list. No e-visa or ETA system found on any official Gambia source. NOTE on UK nationals: UK FCDO states the arrival stamp is 28 days (extendable twice by 28 days at Immigration Office); Gambia official sources state the overall maximum is 90 days - the 28-day stamp is the initial immigration grant, extendable to 90 days total.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomats and Consular Corps are explicitly listed among visa-exempt categories in the Feb 2026 'New Visa Policy' document (Section I: 'DIPLOMATS AND CONSULAR CORPS'). Section IV states: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states including Great Britain and Switzerland, Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators holding diplomatic, official/service or ordinary passports can travel to The Gambia visa free and are not required to be in possession of clearance.' Entry conditions: must possess valid travel document, enter through a recognised entry point, have visible means of sustenance, must not be a prohibited immigrant.

credential.subtype: Conventional Travel Document for Refugees (1951 Refugee Convention)

conditional_access.conditions: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'HOLDERS OF CONVENTIONAL CARD FOR REFUGEES'). Entry conditions: must be in possession of a valid travel document, enter through a recognised entry point, have visible means of sustenance, must not be in the category of prohibited immigrants. Free entry for maximum of 90 days.

credential.subtype: Official travel document / laissez-passer from international organisation

conditional_access.conditions: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'WORKERS OF INTERNATIONAL ORGANISATIONS SUCH AS; UN, AU, EU, IMF ETC' and Section II): 'Holders of official travel documents from the following international organisations can visit The Gambia visa-free: United Nations (UN) and its specialised agencies, African Union Commission (AU), ECOWAS Commission, African Development Bank.' Entry conditions: must possess a mission order or visa waiver; must hold official travel document or visitors pass/laissez-passer.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term leisure or holiday visits to The Gambia. Allows tourism and informal business visits not exceeding the permitted stay.
visa_types.notes: Visa obtainable at embassies/consulates abroad or on arrival at Banjul International Airport. US State Dept notes on-arrival cost is $100–105 (cash only, USD or dalasi). Embassy fee quoted as $0 for single-entry tourist visa when processed via consulate for some nationalities. An airport security fee of $20 USD is additionally required on both entry and departure. Countries needing clearance must obtain pre-arrival clearance before travelling. 90-day maximum stay per gid.gov.gm and gambia.gov.gm.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry leisure or holiday visits to The Gambia, valid for repeated entry over a longer period.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry tourist visa valid up to 5 years; must be applied for at a Gambian embassy or consulate abroad. Embassy fee approximately $139 USD. Processing 4–10 business days. Maximum single stay 90 days per entry. Airport security fee of $20 USD required on each entry and departure.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visits for business meetings, conferences, trade, and exploration of business opportunities in The Gambia.
visa_types.notes: Business visa issued for a maximum stay of 30 days; validity period up to 5 years (multiple entry). Embassy fee approximately $139 USD. Also obtainable on arrival at Banjul International Airport per gid.gov.gm (same on-arrival window as tourist). Applicants must present business documentation and a valid passport with at least 6 months remaining validity. gambia.gov.gm confirms business visits up to 90 days are also covered under the general visa-exemption framework for exempt nationalities.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay transit through The Gambia for travellers en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Embassy fee is $0 for transit visa (consular processing). Transit passengers are exempt from the $20 airport security fee. Applicants must hold onward ticket to a third country, valid visa for final destination if required, and proof of sufficient funds for the layover period. Single entry only.

visa_types.name: Missionary Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visits for religious or faith-based missionary work in The Gambia.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Gambian embassy or consulate abroad. Embassy fee approximately $139 USD; valid up to 5 years, multiple entry. Applicants must submit a letter of invitation from their sponsor or host organisation in Gambia detailing purpose, length of stay, accommodation, and contact information.

visa_types.name: Official/Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for government officials and diplomatic personnel travelling to The Gambia on official business.
visa_types.notes: The Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document lists 'Diplomats and Consular Corps' as a visa-exempt category. Holders of diplomatic, official/service, or ordinary passports from EU states including UK and Switzerland are visa-free per Section IV. Countries with diplomatic visa-waiver agreements are handled bilaterally. Specific fee and processing information not published on accessible official sources.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists. Per gid.gov.gm/citizenship/, citizenship is acquired only by birth, descent, marriage (after 7 years ordinary residence), or naturalization (after 15 continuous years ordinary residence). The 1997 Constitution of Gambia explicitly permits dual citizenship, though dual citizens cannot contest the presidency, National Assembly elections, or serve in the military.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) - free movement of persons

validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-13 against live official sources: gid.gov.gm/visa/, gid.gov.gm/entry-requirements/, gambia.gov.gm/immigration-and-visas/, gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/, and the Feb 2026 HC Senegal New Visa Policy PDF.

CORRECTION: Changed source_official from false to true on all three conditional_access entries. The Gambia High Commission in Senegal is an official diplomatic mission of the Gambia government; its policy PDF is an official government document.

ADDITION: Added Switzerland (CHE) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: SCHENGEN/EU including Switzerland; Section IV explicitly names Switzerland). Switzerland was absent from gid.gov.gm enumerated list and from the prior record.

ADDITION: Added United Arab Emirates (ARE) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS). This CONFLICTS with gid.gov.gm which lists UAE under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance'. Newer document recorded; conflict flagged in notes.

ADDITION: Added Turkey (TUR) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS). Turkey did not appear in any category on gid.gov.gm/visa/ in the prior record - it was only mentioned in data_quality.gaps.

ADDITION: Added 'African Union member states (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: AU CITIZENS). This conflicts with the gid.gov.gm country-level list which places many African countries under 'visa + clearance required'.
ADDITION: Added 'Nordic States (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: NORDIC STATES).

ADDITION: Added 'Baltic and Balkan States (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: BALTIC AND BALKAN STATES). Conflicts with gid.gov.gm which places several Baltic/Balkan countries under 'visa + clearance required'.
ADDITION: Added 'Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I and Section IV).
UPDATE: Added gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ as an additional confirmed source URL (separate from /immigration-and-visas/ - both pages exist and are live).
UPDATE: Expanded source_urls list to include both gambia.gov.gm URL variants and the HC Senegal PDF.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: The 18 countries in the gid.gov.gm visa exemption agreement list are exactly as previously recorded. ECOWAS free-entry list (11 countries) confirmed unchanged. Mauritania/Mali/Niger/Burkina Faso privilege note confirmed unchanged. No e-visa or ETA system found. CBI/RBI/fast_track: no programs found, confirmed unchanged.

Re-validated 2026-06-27 against live official sources: gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ (loaded, confirmed UK/EU/Commonwealth/ECOWAS visa-free up to 90 days); gambiahc.org.uk/consular-services/visa-application/ (loaded, confirmed UK/Commonwealth/ECOWAS/EU visa-free up to 90 days); gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/the-gambia/entry-requirements (loaded, confirmed UK nationals visa-free, initial 28-day stamp extendable twice by 28 days to max 84 days per FCDO guidance, but Gambia official policy ceiling is 90 days).

NOTE 2026-06-27: gid.gov.gm/visa/ and gid.gov.gm/entry-requirements/ returned SSL certificate errors and could not be loaded. The detailed per-country category list (ECOWAS, visa exemption agreements, clearance-required, visa-only) could not be re-confirmed from live gid.gov.gm pages. All gid.gov.gm-sourced entries preserved per safety rules.

NOTE 2026-06-27: The Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (gambiahcsenegal.com) loaded as binary and could not be parsed as text. All entries sourced from this document preserved per safety rules and noted as 'could not re-confirm from live readable source in 2026-06-27 re-validation'.

NOTE 2026-06-27: UK FCDO guidance clarified: British nationals receive a 28-day arrival stamp, extendable twice by 28 days each at Immigration Office (total maximum 84 days per extension path, or 90 days per Gambia official policy). Updated UK entry notes and added gov.uk source URL. No other changes to nationality access lists were warranted.
UPDATE 2026-06-27: Added gambiahc.org.uk to official_domains and source_urls.

Re-validated 2026-06-29 against live official sources: gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ (loaded, confirmed UK/EU/Commonwealth/ECOWAS + reciprocal agreement nations visa-free up to 90 days); gambia.gov.gm/immigration-and-visas/ (loaded, confirmed same groups); gambiahc.org.uk/consular-services/visa-application/ (loaded, confirmed UK/Commonwealth/ECOWAS/EU visa-free 90 days); gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/the-gambia/entry-requirements (loaded, UK nationals: 28-day initial stamp, extendable twice by 28 days at Immigration Office or tourist police stations = max 84 days via extension route; longer stays require a residence permit - contact Gambia High Commission UK); travel.state.gov (loaded, US citizens need a visa, obtainable on arrival at Banjul International Airport, fee $100-105 cash only USD or dalasi, $20 security fee, max stay 30 days); gambiahcsenegal.com/visas/ (loaded, confirms visa exemption framework but no country list visible in page text); gambiahcsenegal.com HC Senegal PDF (loaded as binary again, text still unreadable - all entries sourced from this document preserved per safety rules).
NOTE 2026-06-29: gid.gov.gm/visa/ and gid.gov.gm/entry-requirements/ returned SSL certificate errors again - could not be loaded. All gid.gov.gm-sourced entries preserved per safety rules.

NOTE 2026-06-29: UK FCDO (gov.uk) now confirms max stay through extensions is 84 days (28 + 28 + 28); for stays beyond this a residence permit is required. Gambia's official policy ceiling remains stated as 90 days on gambia.gov.gm. The discrepancy is unresolved. UK notes in visa_free already capture this; no change to max_stay_days (kept at 90 per Gambia stated policy) but gap entry updated to reflect FCDO stance.

NOTE 2026-06-29: US citizens confirmed to require a visa (on arrival) by US State Department. This is consistent with the gid.gov.gm categorisation that US appears in the 'only visa' category. US is NOT currently in the visa_on_arrival list - gid.gov.gm (the Gambia official source) is unreachable due to SSL errors and cannot confirm the specific US VOA status. US added to data_quality.gaps as a probable VOA candidate with 30-day max stay, pending confirmation from a live official Gambia source.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED 2026-06-29: visa_free list (ECOWAS 15 countries, 18 named exemption-agreement countries, group labels for EU/Commonwealth/AU/Nordic/Baltic-Balkan/Chartered Flights, Switzerland/UAE/Turkey from HC Senegal PDF), visa_on_arrival (general provision), e_visa (empty), eta (empty), cbi (no program), rbi (no programs), fast_track (no programs). No additions or removals to nationality access lists this run - no live official Gambia sources confirmed any change.
gaps: No official e-visa portal or ETA system was found on any Gambia government source.
No official RBI, golden visa, or investor residence program was found on any official source.
No official fast-track, digital-nomad, startup, or skilled worker visa scheme was found on any official source.

Indonesia and Brunei Darussalam appear in both the 'Countries With Visa Exemption Agreement' list and the 'clearance-only' list on gid.gov.gm/visa/ - an inconsistency on the official page; visa-exemption agreement entry is recorded in visa_free.

Several entries in the Pacific visa-only category (Flores, Lombok, Sulawesi, Sumbawa, Tasmania, Melanesia) appear to be Indonesian/Australian geographic regions rather than sovereign states - these appear to be listing errors on the official page and have not been recorded as separate entries.

The Feb 2026 HC Senegal New Visa Policy PDF creates major conflicts with the gid.gov.gm/visa/ enumerated list: (a) UAE listed as visa-exempt in PDF but as 'visa + clearance required' on gid.gov.gm; (b) 'AU CITIZENS' visa-exempt in PDF but many specific African countries listed as needing 'visa + clearance' on gid.gov.gm; (c) 'Baltic and Balkan States' visa-exempt in PDF but Estonia, Lithuania, Albania, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Georgia, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine listed as needing 'visa + clearance' on gid.gov.gm. It is unclear which document represents current operative policy.

The exact nature of 'entry clearance' (whether a pre-arrival administrative permission or a form of visa) is not fully explained on official pages. The GID entry-requirements page clarifies it requires a host to take up immigration responsibilities in a prescribed form and must be obtained before travel.
mofa.gov.gm (Ministry of Foreign Affairs) was not reachable in 2026-06-27 re-validation - could not be checked for diplomatic passport waiver agreements or any updated visa policy announcements.

No third-country credential-based conditional rules found on any official source - i.e., no rule granting entry based on holding a US, UK, Schengen, or other foreign visa, residence permit, or permanent residence card. No OCI cardholder rule found.
No specific bilateral diplomatic visa waiver agreements with named countries and dates were found on accessible official sources.

gambia.gov.gm immigration page states tourists on last-minute bookings 'will be allowed entry but will be required to submit their passport to the Department of Immigration in Banjul within 48 hours to be issued a proper visa' - the nationality scope of this provision is unclear.

The Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF also lists 'SCHENGEN... CITIZENS' as visa-exempt in Section I, but the Schengen Area includes some non-EU states (Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein) - the overlap with separately listed Nordic States and Switzerland creates ambiguity about exact scope.

France, Austria, Portugal, Malta, Greece, Hungary, Romania appear in gid.gov.gm 'Only Visa' (European) list but are EU members covered by the EU group label - these may represent an outdated list or countries that require an individual visa despite the EU exemption, the conflict is unresolved.

gid.gov.gm SSL certificate error as of 2026-06-27 - the detailed per-country visa category tables on the official immigration department site cannot be read. All entries sourced from gid.gov.gm are preserved but could not be re-confirmed against a live page this run.

The HC Senegal PDF could not be parsed as readable text in 2026-06-27 re-validation. Entries sourced exclusively from this document (UAE, Turkey, Switzerland, AU group, Nordic group, Baltic/Balkan group, Chartered Flights group) could not be re-confirmed from a live readable source this run.

Discrepancy: UK FCDO states initial arrival stamp is 28 days (max 84 days with two extensions, after which a residence permit is required); Gambia official policy states 90 days maximum. The precise relationship between the 28-day stamp practice and the 90-day policy ceiling is not definitively explained on any official Gambia government page. As of 2026-06-29, FCDO confirms the cap is 84 days via the stamp+extension path.

USA visa-on-arrival: US State Department (travel.state.gov, loaded 2026-06-29) confirms US citizens need a visa to enter Gambia, obtainable on arrival at Banjul International Airport; fee $100-105 cash only; max stay 30 days; $20 security fee on arrival and departure. However, gid.gov.gm (the relevant official Gambia source that categorises US under 'visa-only') is unreachable due to SSL errors and cannot be read from a live page. USA is not currently listed in visa_on_arrival. Per safety rules, USA is NOT added until a live official Gambia government source confirms the VOA mechanism specifically.

EFTA group: Wikipedia's visa policy of Gambia article lists 'All European Free Trade Association member states' as visa-free. This is not confirmed by any live official Gambia government source read in any validation run. EFTA members (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland) - Iceland and Switzerland are individually listed in the existing record; Norway may be covered by the Nordic States group label. Liechtenstein status unconfirmed from any official source.
